axis: temporal / system-versioning (convert one physical table to SYSTEM_VERSIONED; check whether each engine emits the temporal shape or a plain table)
provisioned: Database ParityAxis_temporal. Loaded the base OSSYS edge-case seed (3 ossys_* metamodel tables + 14 OSUSR_* physical tables), then converted the City entity's physical table OSUSR_DEF_CITY into a SYSTEM_VERSIONED temporal table: ALTER TABLE ... ADD [SYSSTART] DATETIME2(7) GENERATED ALWAYS AS ROW START HIDDEN NOT NULL DEFAULT SYSUTCDATETIME(), [SYSEND] DATETIME2(7) GENERATED ALWAYS AS ROW END HIDDEN NOT NULL DEFAULT '9999-12-31...', PERIOD FOR SYSTEM_TIME([SYSSTART],[SYSEND]); then SET (SYSTEM_VERSIONING = ON (HISTORY_TABLE=[dbo].[OSUSR_DEF_CITY_HISTORY])). No ossys_Entity_Attr rows added for the period columns on purpose — SQL Server period columns are physical-only, not OutSystems attributes, mirroring real estates. SOURCE TRUTH via sys-catalog: OSUSR_DEF_CITY temporal_type=2 (SYSTEM_VERSIONED_TEMPORAL_TABLE), OSUSR_DEF_CITY_HISTORY temporal_type=1 (HISTORY_TABLE); sys.periods = SYSTEM_TIME(SYSSTART,SYSEND); SYSSTART=AS_ROW_START/hidden, SYSEND=AS_ROW_END/hidden. Both CLIs exited 0 (v1 full-export exit 0; v2 publish exit 0, 12 stages "succeeded"). Deploy targets ParityAxis_temporal_V1 / _V2. All three databases dropped at end (verified none remain).
v1Result: v1 full-export --profiler-provider sql, exit 0. The extracted model.json contains ZERO temporal metadata (0 "temporal" keys; no OSUSR_DEF_CITY_HISTORY; no SYSSTART/SYSEND) — the V1 SQL extraction (outsystems_model_export.sql / outsystems_metadata_rowsets.sql) reads no temporal_type / sys.periods. Emitted Modules/AppCore/dbo.City.sql is a PLAIN table: `CREATE TABLE [dbo].[City] ([Id] BIGINT IDENTITY (1,1) NOT NULL CONSTRAINT [PK_City_Id] PRIMARY KEY CLUSTERED, [Name] NVARCHAR (200) NOT NULL, [IsActive] BIT NOT NULL CONSTRAINT [DF_OSUSR_DEF_CITY_ISACTIVE] DEFAULT 1)` — no PERIOD FOR SYSTEM_TIME, no SYSTEM_VERSIONING, no GENERATED ALWAYS columns, no history table. DEPLOY to ParityAxis_temporal_V1: sqlcmd exit 0; sys.tables City temporal_type=0 (NON_TEMPORAL_TABLE), 0 periods. No temporal diagnostic emitted. (V1 owns a TemporalTableMetadata domain model + TemporalMetadataMapper JSON deserializer + a HasTemporalHistory coverage predicate, but those consume model-JSON input only; the SSDT table writers — TablePlanWriter/SsdtEmitter/TableEmissionPlan/TableHeaderFactory — contain no temporal/PERIOD/SYSTEM_VERSIONING/GENERATED-ALWAYS emit code, so there is no path from a live temporal source to temporal DDL.)
v2Result: v2 publish (profiler live), exit 0. catalog.snapshot.json: City kind modality=[] and the string "Temporal" is absent from the entire catalog (no SYSSTART, no OSUSR_DEF_CITY_HISTORY). Emitted Modules/AppCore/dbo.City.sql is a PLAIN table with the same CREATE TABLE shape as V1 plus 8 sp_addextendedproperty (Projection.LogicalName / Projection.SsKey) statements — no PERIOD, no SYSTEM_VERSIONING, no GENERATED ALWAYS columns, no history table. DEPLOY to ParityAxis_temporal_V2: sqlcmd exit 0; sys.tables City temporal_type=0 (NON_TEMPORAL_TABLE), 0 periods. No temporal diagnostic (the only 2 "temporal" hits in the run log were my database name in a file path). KEY: V2 DOES carry a complete temporal emit path — ScriptDomBuild.fs:768-794 emits `PERIOD FOR SYSTEM_TIME` + `WITH (SYSTEM_VERSIONING = ON ...)` + HISTORY_RETENTION_PERIOD whenever a Kind carries ModalityMark.Temporal; SsdtDdlEmitter.fs:385-389 threads TemporalConfig into Statement.CreateTable; CatalogCodec + ManifestEmitter handle it. But the sole data-constructor of ModalityMark.Temporal is the catalog-snapshot JSON reader (CatalogCodec.fs:754, a self round-trip). Neither live-model adapter marks it: OssysRowsetReader.fs:651-655 yields only `Static []`/`SystemOwned`, OssysJsonReader.fs:617 yields only `Static []`; KindRow (MetadataSnapshotRunner.fs:1311-1336) has no temporal field; the OssysSql extraction rowset SQL reads no temporal_type/sys.periods. The emit machinery is therefore unreachable from any real OutSystems estate.

findings.topic: Emitted table shape for a SYSTEM_VERSIONED source (does the engine emit PERIOD + SYSTEM_VERSIONING + GENERATED ALWAYS, or a plain table?)
findings.v1: Plain CREATE TABLE [dbo].[City] — no PERIOD FOR SYSTEM_TIME, no SYSTEM_VERSIONING, no GENERATED ALWAYS period cols, no history table; deployed temporal_type=0.
findings.v2: Plain CREATE TABLE [dbo].[City] (+8 logical-name extended props) — no PERIOD/SYSTEM_VERSIONING/GENERATED ALWAYS, no history table; deployed temporal_type=0.
findings.difference: NONE — perfect parity, and both are wrong the same way: a source table with temporal_type=2 is emitted by both engines as a plain temporal_type=0 table. Both silently drop the two GENERATED ALWAYS ROW START/END hidden columns (SYSSTART/SYSEND), the PERIOD FOR SYSTEM_TIME, the SYSTEM_VERSIONING option, and the OSUSR_DEF_CITY_HISTORY history table. Neither engine emits any warning/diagnostic about the loss.
findings.proof: SOURCE sys.tables: OSUSR_DEF_CITY temporal_type=2 (SYSTEM_VERSIONED_TEMPORAL_TABLE), OSUSR_DEF_CITY_HISTORY=1 (HISTORY_TABLE); sys.periods SYSTEM_TIME(SYSSTART,SYSEND). V1 & V2 dbo.City.sql both = `CREATE TABLE [dbo].[City] ([Id] BIGINT IDENTITY (1,1) NOT NULL CONSTRAINT [PK_City_Id] PRIMARY KEY CLUSTERED, [Name] NVARCHAR (200) NOT NULL, [IsActive] BIT NOT NULL CONSTRAINT [DF_OSUSR_DEF_CITY_ISACTIVE] DEFAULT 1)`. Deployed reality: ParityAxis_temporal_V1 City temporal_type=0 / 0 periods; ParityAxis_temporal_V2 City temporal_type=0 / 0 periods; both sqlcmd deploys exit 0.

findings.topic: C10 claim — V2 has temporal emit support but no adapter marks it, so it emits a plain table silently
findings.v1: No temporal emit path at all: extraction reads no temporal (model.json has 0 temporal keys) and the SSDT table writers contain zero temporal-emit code. Temporal exists only as a model-JSON-input domain model + a HasTemporalHistory coverage predicate.
findings.v2: A COMPLETE temporal emit path exists (ScriptDomBuild.fs:768-794 emits PERIOD FOR SYSTEM_TIME + WITH(SYSTEM_VERSIONING=ON) + HISTORY_RETENTION_PERIOD) but is dead code on the live path — no adapter ever constructs ModalityMark.Temporal from an estate.
findings.difference: C10 CONFIRMED empirically. V2 is architecturally capable of emitting the temporal shape, but the only data-constructor of ModalityMark.Temporal is the catalog-snapshot JSON reader (CatalogCodec.fs:754, i.e. re-reading V2's own emitted catalog). Both live-model adapters yield only Static/SystemOwned marks, and KindRow/extraction-SQL carry no temporal field. Net realized output is byte-for-byte the same plain table as V1 — the divergence is latent capability, not output: V2's temporal support is unreachable, V1's is absent.
findings.proof: Empirical: v2 catalog.snapshot.json City modality=[]; substring 'Temporal' absent from whole catalog; emitted City.sql plain; deployed temporal_type=0. Code trace: emit present at ScriptDomBuild.fs:768-794 & SsdtDdlEmitter.fs:385-389 (`ModalityMark.Temporal tc -> Some tc`); sole data-constructor at CatalogCodec.fs:754 (`"Temporal" -> ... ModalityMark.Temporal`); live adapters at OssysRowsetReader.fs:653-654 (`if kindRow.IsStatic then yield Static []; if kindRow.IsSystemEntity then yield SystemOwned`) and OssysJsonReader.fs:617 (`if isStatic then [ Static [] ] else []`) — neither yields Temporal; KindRow at MetadataSnapshotRunner.fs:1311-1336 has no temporal field.

findings.topic: Incidental (off-axis) difference observed on the same plain table: extended-property emission
findings.v1: Emits the bare CREATE TABLE only (8-line file), no extended properties.
findings.v2: Emits the same CREATE TABLE plus 8 EXECUTE sp_addextendedproperty calls (Projection.LogicalName + Projection.SsKey, at table and column grain).
findings.difference: Not temporal-specific — a pre-existing general v1/v2 emission difference that happens to appear in the City table used for this axis. Both deploy cleanly (exit 0) and both are temporal_type=0, so it does not affect the temporal verdict.
findings.proof: V2 dbo.City.sql lines 12-63 contain 8 `EXECUTE [sys].[sp_addextendedproperty]` statements; V1 dbo.City.sql is 8 lines total with none. Both deployed to temporal_type=0.

provisioned: DB ParityAxis_computedpersisted: created + loaded base OSSYS seed (15 entities / 7 modules), then ALTER TABLE dbo.OSUSR_INV_STOCKITEM ADD [TOTALVALUE] AS ([WAREHOUSEQTY]*[AVGCOST]) PERSISTED, [LABEL] AS (CONVERT(NVARCHAR(20),[REORDERLEVEL])) [non-persisted]; plus a physical-only probe [SHADOWCALC] AS ([REORDERLEVEL]*2) PERSISTED with NO ossys_Entity_Attr row; and matching ossys_Entity_Attr rows 60008 (TotalValue, Float) + 60009 (Label, Text). Source truth verified via sys.computed_columns on OSUSR_INV_STOCKITEM: TOTALVALUE is_persisted=1 def=([WAREHOUSEQTY]*[AVGCOST]); LABEL is_persisted=0 def=(CONVERT([nvarchar](20),[REORDERLEVEL])); SHADOWCALC is_persisted=1. Seed baseline OSUSR_SAL_PRODUCT.DISPLAYLABEL is_persisted=1 def=(concat([SKU],N'-',CONVERT([nvarchar](20),[PRICE]))). Server/default collation SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS. BOTH CLIs exited 0: v1 full-export rc=0; v2 publish 12/12 stages "succeeded". Deploy targets _V1 / _V2 (CI collation) + an extra _CS (SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CS_AS) for the case-sensitivity hazard test. All four DBs dropped at end.
v1Result: v1 full-export (extract->model.json->build SSDT) surfaced and emitted BOTH attr-backed computed columns, REWRITING the expression identifiers physical->logical to match its renamed columns: Modules/Inventory/dbo.StockItem.sql -> "[TotalValue] AS ([WarehouseQty] * [AvgCost])" and "[Label] AS (CONVERT (NVARCHAR (20), [ReorderLevel]))"; Modules/Sales/dbo.Product.sql -> "[DisplayLabel] AS (concat([Sku], N'-', CONVERT (NVARCHAR (20), [Price])))". NO PERSISTED keyword on any (SMO model has no persisted concept; model.json onDisk carries isComputed:true + computedDefinition but no persisted field). SHADOWCALC (no attr row) NOT emitted. Deploy: _V1 (CI) exit 0; deployed sys.computed_columns -> TotalValue is_persisted=0 def=([WarehouseQty]*[AvgCost]), Label is_persisted=0, DisplayLabel is_persisted=0. Extra CS-collation deploy of v1 StockItem.sql: exit 0 (identifiers match columns).
v2Result: v2 publish (model.ossys live) surfaced and emitted BOTH attr-backed computed columns but emitted the expression VERBATIM from sys.computed_columns.definition (uppercase physical, NO identifier rewrite): dist/onprem/Modules/Inventory/dbo.StockItem.sql -> "[TotalValue] AS ([WAREHOUSEQTY] * [AVGCOST])" and "[Label] AS (CONVERT (NVARCHAR (20), [REORDERLEVEL]))"; dist/onprem/Modules/Sales/dbo.Product.sql -> "[DisplayLabel] AS (concat([SKU], N'-', CONVERT (NVARCHAR (20), [PRICE])))". NO PERSISTED (catalog.snapshot.json isPersisted:false; OssysRowsetReader.fs:139 hardcodes ComputedColumnConfig.create expr false with a code comment naming the gap; ScriptDomBuild.fs:441 would emit PERSISTED only if IsPersisted, which is always false). Columns themselves renamed to logical mixed-case ([WarehouseQty] etc.) just like v1. SHADOWCALC NOT emitted. Deploy: _V2 (CI) exit 0; deployed sys.computed_columns -> TotalValue is_persisted=0 def=([WAREHOUSEQTY]*[AVGCOST]), Label is_persisted=0, DisplayLabel is_persisted=0 (SQL Server preserved the as-written uppercase casing). Extra CS-collation deploy of v2 StockItem.sql: FAILED Msg 207 Level 16 "Invalid column name 'WAREHOUSEQTY'" + "'AVGCOST'"; table not created.

findings.topic: Attr-backed computed column emission
findings.v1: Emits TotalValue, Label, and baseline DisplayLabel as AS-expressions; deploys on CI collation
findings.v2: Emits TotalValue, Label, and baseline DisplayLabel as AS-expressions; deploys on CI collation
findings.difference: No difference in WHETHER the computed column is surfaced/emitted. Any physical computed column that has a matching ossys_Entity_Attr row is emitted by both engines as a computed (AS ...) column with no explicit datatype.
findings.proof: Both output trees contain '[TotalValue] AS (...)', '[Label] AS (...)', '[DisplayLabel] AS (...)'; both deployed to _V1/_V2 (CI collation) exit 0 with all three present in sys.computed_columns.

findings.topic: PERSISTED flag preservation
findings.v1: Drops PERSISTED: emits no PERSISTED keyword; SMO model (SmoModel.cs:55) has only IsComputed/ComputedExpression, no persisted field
findings.v2: Drops PERSISTED: IR has ComputedColumnConfig.IsPersisted but OssysRowsetReader.fs:139 hardcodes it false (comment: 'V1 SQL doesn't surface sys.computed_columns.is_persisted')
findings.difference: Both engines LOSE the PERSISTED attribute. Source TOTALVALUE and DISPLAYLABEL are is_persisted=1; both engines emit them non-persisted. Source LABEL (is_persisted=0) is correctly non-persisted in both, so the loss is specifically the persisted->non-persisted downgrade. Root cause is shared: neither outsystems_metadata_rowsets.sql selects sys.computed_columns.is_persisted (verified identical column-reality section in both files).
findings.proof: Deployed sys.computed_columns.is_persisted=0 for TotalValue AND DisplayLabel in BOTH _V1 and _V2; source truth is_persisted=1 for both. Source LABEL is_persisted=0 -> both deployed is_persisted=0 (parity, correct). v1 model.json TotalValue.onDisk has no persisted key; v2 catalog.snapshot.json shows isPersisted:false.

findings.topic: Computed-expression column-identifier rewrite (physical->logical)
findings.v1: REWRITES identifiers to the logical column names: emits ([WarehouseQty] * [AvgCost]), (CONVERT(NVARCHAR(20),[ReorderLevel])), (concat([Sku],...[Price])) via CreateTableStatementBuilder RewriteColumnReferences over columnNameMap
findings.v2: Does NOT rewrite: emits the raw sys.computed_columns.definition verbatim ([WAREHOUSEQTY] * [AVGCOST]), (...[REORDERLEVEL]), (concat([SKU],...[PRICE])) via ScriptDomBuild.fs:440 parseComputedExpression
findings.difference: Both rename the TABLE columns to logical mixed-case ([WarehouseQty], [AvgCost], [ReorderLevel], [Sku], [Price]), but only v1 rewrites the computed-EXPRESSION identifiers to match. v2 leaves the uppercase physical identifiers, producing an emitted table whose computed expression references columns by a different case than the columns are declared. The divergence persists post-deploy: SQL Server stores the definition with the as-written casing (it does not re-normalize).
findings.proof: v1 emit '[TotalValue] AS ([WarehouseQty] * [AvgCost])' vs v2 emit '[TotalValue] AS ([WAREHOUSEQTY] * [AVGCOST])'. Deployed sys.computed_columns.definition: _V1=([WarehouseQty]*[AvgCost]) ; _V2=([WAREHOUSEQTY]*[AVGCOST]). Same divergence on Label ([ReorderLevel] vs [REORDERLEVEL]) and DisplayLabel ([Sku]/[Price] vs [SKU]/[PRICE]).

findings.topic: v2 computed DDL is not deployable on a case-sensitive collation
findings.v1: v1 StockItem.sql deploys into a CS-collation DB successfully (exit 0) because its expression identifiers match the declared logical columns exactly
findings.v2: v2 StockItem.sql FAILS to deploy into a CS-collation DB: the un-rewritten [WAREHOUSEQTY]/[AVGCOST] do not bind to columns [WarehouseQty]/[AvgCost]
findings.difference: Direct functional consequence of F3. On the default case-insensitive server collation (SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS) both engines deploy fine, so on this estate it is latent. On any case-sensitive database/column collation, v2's output is a hard deploy failure (blocker) while v1's is robust. This is the strongest empirical signal separating the two engines on this axis.
findings.proof: Deploying v2 dbo.StockItem.sql into DB collated SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CS_AS returned 'Msg 207, Level 16, State 1 ... Invalid column name WAREHOUSEQTY.' and 'Invalid column name AVGCOST.'; OBJECT_ID('dbo.StockItem') was NULL afterward (table not created). v1 dbo.StockItem.sql into the same CS DB returned exit 0.

findings.topic: Physical-only computed column with no metamodel row (extractor blind spot)
findings.v1: Does not surface SHADOWCALC (no ossys_Entity_Attr row): extraction JOINs #Attr to sys.columns by name
findings.v2: Does not surface SHADOWCALC (no ossys_Entity_Attr row): same #Attr-driven column-reality JOIN
findings.difference: A physical computed column that lacks a matching ossys_Entity_Attr row is invisible to BOTH engines. Both are metamodel-driven (the column-reality rowset is anchored on the OSSYS attribute set), so a DB-only computed column is never emitted. Parity, and by-design for an OutSystems-metamodel exporter.
findings.proof: Source sys.columns shows SHADOWCALC with IsComputed=1 (is_persisted=1) but no ossys_Entity_Attr row; grep for SHADOWCALC across both full output trees (v1/out and v2/dist) returns zero matches.

axis: empty-string vs NULL on the data plane (packet F11) — static-seed data lane
provisioned: DB: ParityAxis_emptystringnull (seeded from ossys-edge-case.seed.sql; +_V1/_V2 as deploy targets). Added a NULLABLE text column [NICKNAME] NVARCHAR(50) NULL to the static entity Country (OSUSR_REF_COUNTRY) plus a matching ossys_Entity_Attr row (Id=70004, Entity_Id=7000, Data_Type='Text', Length=50, Is_Mandatory=0, Is_Active=1, Physical_Column_Name='NICKNAME'). Inserted 6 rows (IDENTITY_INSERT) exercising the full matrix — 1/USA NICKNAME='' (empty on NULLABLE text); 2/GBR NAME='' (empty on NOT NULL text); 3/FRA NICKNAME=NULL (SQL NULL on nullable); 4/DEU NICKNAME=' ' (single space on nullable — sentinel candidate); 5/JPN NAME=' ' (single space on NOT NULL/mandatory); 6/ITA genuine control. Source truth confirmed via DATALENGTH/IS NULL sys-catalog SELECT (e.g. USA NICKNAME DATALENGTH=0 not-null; GBR NAME DATALENGTH=0 not-null; DEU NICKNAME DATALENGTH=2 not-null). Both CLIs exited 0: v1 full-export exit 0, v2 publish exit 0 (emit+store succeeded).
v1Result: v1 full-export emitted Seeds/RefData/StaticEntities.seed.sql (SqlStaticEntityDataProvider live read, StaticEntitySeedColumn.NormalizeValue + SqlLiteralFormatter). Exact VALUES: `MERGE INTO [dbo].[Country] AS Target USING ( VALUES (1, N'USA', N'United States', N''), (2, N'GBR', N'', N'Britain'), (3, N'FRA', N'France', NULL), (4, N'DEU', N'Germany', NULL), (5, N'JPN', N' ', N'Japan'), (6, N'ITA', N'Italy', N'Italia') ) AS Source ([Id], [Code], [Name], [Nickname])`. Empty string '' preserved as N'' on BOTH the nullable NICKNAME (row 1) and the NOT NULL NAME (row 2); NULL preserved (row 3); single-space ' ' on nullable NICKNAME normalized to NULL (row 4); single-space ' ' on mandatory NAME preserved as N' ' (row 5). Deploy: CREATE TABLE [dbo].[Country] + seed against ParityAxis_emptystringnull_V1 → DDL exit 0, seed exit 0, no Msg errors; the NOT NULL NAME='' inserted cleanly.
v2Result: v2 publish emitted Data/StaticSeeds.sql (StaticSeedsEmitter → KindColumns.outSystemsSpaceSentinel + SqlLiteral.ofRaw). Exact VALUES: `MERGE INTO [dbo].[Country] AS [Target] USING (VALUES (1, N'USA', N'United States', N''), (2, N'GBR', N'', N'Britain'), (3, N'FRA', N'France', NULL), (4, N'DEU', N'Germany', NULL), (5, N'JPN', N' ', N'Japan'), (6, N'ITA', N'Italy', N'Italia')) AS [Source]([Id], [Code], [Name], [Nickname])`. Per-cell literal sequence is BYTE-IDENTICAL to v1 (normalized diff: empty/no differences). Empty '' → N'' on nullable (row 1) AND NOT NULL (row 2) — the hypothesized universal ''-as-NULL sentinel is NOT applied; NULL → NULL (row 3); single-space ' ' on nullable → NULL (row 4, sentinel); single-space on mandatory → N' ' (row 5). Deploy: CREATE TABLE [dbo].[Country] + seed against ParityAxis_emptystringnull_V2 → DDL exit 0, seed exit 0 (6 rows affected), no Msg errors; NOT NULL NAME='' inserted cleanly.

findings.topic: Empty string '' on text columns — universal ''-as-NULL sentinel
findings.v1: '' emitted as N'' on both nullable NICKNAME (row 1/USA) and NOT NULL NAME (row 2/GBR)
findings.v2: '' emitted as N'' on both nullable NICKNAME (row 1/USA) and NOT NULL NAME (row 2/GBR) — identical
findings.difference: None. The axis hypothesis (that v2 collapses '' to NULL via an F11 universal sentinel) is empirically REFUTED. v2 preserves the genuine empty string exactly as v1 does. The old NM-18 universal ''-as-NULL sentinel was deliberately retired (WP-3); NULL is now carried out-of-band as None, and Some "" is a faithful Text value rendered N''.
findings.proof: Both engines' VALUES tuples 1 and 2 are byte-identical: `(1, N'USA', N'United States', N'')` and `(2, N'GBR', N'', N'Britain')`. Code: v2 SqlLiteral.ofRaw Text (Some "") -> TextLit "" -> N'' (SqlLiteral.fs:145,184-198); read side maps IsDBNull->ValueNone and preserves '' as ValueSome "" (ReadSide.fs:909-915); Catalog.fs:78-79 documents 'the retired universal ""-as-NULL sentinel'. v1 SqlLiteralFormatter.FormatValue("")=>N'' (SqlLiteralFormatter.cs:18). Deployed readback: USA NICKNAME DATALENGTH=0 not-null and GBR NAME DATALENGTH=0 not-null on BOTH V1 and V2.

findings.topic: NOT NULL text column carrying '' — does the data load fail?
findings.v1: NAME='' (NOT NULL) loads successfully; stored DATALENGTH=0, not null
findings.v2: NAME='' (NOT NULL) loads successfully; stored DATALENGTH=0, not null
findings.difference: None. Neither engine's load fails on a NOT NULL text column carrying ''. An empty string is a valid non-NULL value, so it satisfies the NOT NULL constraint; the axis's failure hypothesis does not materialize on either side.
findings.proof: Row 2 (GBR, NAME='') MERGE deployed against fresh DBs: v1 seed exit 0, v2 seed exit 0 (6 rows affected), zero Msg 515/NULL-insert errors on either target. Readback: Country.Id=2 Name DATALENGTH=0, Name IS NULL=0 on both V1 and V2 (matches source OSUSR_REF_COUNTRY GBR NAME DATALENGTH=0 not-null).

findings.topic: OutSystems single-space (' ') sentinel on NULLABLE text
findings.v1: NICKNAME=' ' (nullable) normalized to NULL (row 4/DEU emits NULL)
findings.v2: NICKNAME=' ' (nullable) normalized to NULL (row 4/DEU emits NULL) — identical
findings.difference: None. Both engines apply the OutSystems single-space -> NULL sentinel identically, gated to nullable text columns. This is the ONE deliberate transformation on the axis and it is at exact parity (v1 StaticEntitySeedColumn.NormalizeValue vs v2 KindColumns.outSystemsSpaceSentinel).
findings.proof: Source DEU NICKNAME DATALENGTH=2 (single NCHAR space), not null. Both emit tuple 4 as `(4, N'DEU', N'Germany', NULL)`. Deployed readback: Country.Id=4 Nickname IS NULL=1 on BOTH V1 and V2 (the only row that diverges from source, and it diverges identically). Code: v1 NormalizeValue nullable+len-1-space=>null (StaticEntitySeedScriptGenerator.cs:236-244); v2 Some " " when Text && not IsMandatory=>None (KindColumns.fs:113-116).

findings.topic: Single-space (' ') on MANDATORY / NOT NULL text (sentinel gating)
findings.v1: NAME=' ' (mandatory) preserved as N' ' (row 5/JPN)
findings.v2: NAME=' ' (mandatory) preserved as N' ' (row 5/JPN) — identical

findings.v2: NICKNAME=NULL emitted as NULL (row 3/FRA) — identical

findings.v2: 6/6 rows round-trip; deployed Country identical to source except the intentional single-space->NULL (row 4) — identical to V1

findings.topic: MERGE envelope formatting around identical VALUES (OFF-AXIS observation)
findings.v1:
Multi-line USING( 
 VALUES 
 (tuple), 
 (tuple) ...) with newline+8-space separators; `AS Target`; `AS Source (...)` with spaces
findings.v2: Single-line `USING (VALUES (tuple), (tuple), ...)` with `, ` inline separators; `AS [Target]`; `AS [Source](...)` bracketed
findings.difference: The MERGE wrapper whitespace/bracketing differs, but every VALUES-cell literal token is byte-identical. This is a MERGE-formatting / emission-aesthetic difference that belongs to a DIFFERENT axis (statement layout, identifier bracketing, extended-property emission in the DDL) — it has zero effect on the empty-string/NULL data-plane semantics and both deploy to identical stored state. Flagged only so the orchestrator does not double-count it against the F11 axis.
findings.proof: v1 tuples separated by ',\n        ' (newline+indent); v2 by ', ' inline. Both deploy exit 0 to byte-identical readback. Also off-axis: v2's Country DDL adds Projection.LogicalName/SsKey extended properties absent in v1 (both tables have identical column shape: Id BIGINT IDENTITY PK, Code NVARCHAR(3) NOT NULL, Name NVARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, Nickname NVARCHAR(50) NULL).

axis: sequences (CREATE SEQUENCE object + sequence-backed DEFAULT NEXT VALUE FOR)

findings.topic: CREATE SEQUENCE object emission from the model-sourced lane
findings.v1: No CREATE SEQUENCE emitted; model.json omits the `sequences` key entirely. Root cause ModelDeserializerFacade.cs:72 hardcodes empty Sequences (no sequence processor in SqlExtraction/).
findings.v2: No CREATE SEQUENCE emitted; catalog.snapshot.json has `sequences: []`. Root cause OssysRowsetReader.fs:951 / OssysJsonReader.fs:756 `Catalog.create modules []`.
findings.difference: Parity: BOTH model-sourced lanes silently omit the CREATE SEQUENCE object for dbo.TestSeq. Directly confirms packet C10 ('sequences silently absent from model-sourced runs'). The source estate's dbo.TestSeq (bigint/1000/5/1/9999999/cycle/cache50) is nowhere in either output.
findings.proof: grep -rin 'CREATE SEQUENCE' returned zero hits in both v1/out and v2/dist. v1 model.json top-level keys = [exportedAtUtc, modules] (no sequences). v2 catalog.snapshot.json top keys include 'sequences' = []. sys.sequences source truth had 1 row (dbo.TestSeq).

findings.topic: Sequence-backed DEFAULT in the realistic OutSystems shape (metamodel Default_Value empty)
findings.v1: Re-emits `DEFAULT  NEXT VALUE FOR [dbo].[TestSeq]` sourced from physical sys.default_constraints (persists with metamodel Default_Value=NULL). Deploy -> Msg 208 Invalid object name 'dbo.TestSeq' (exit 1): NON-DEPLOYABLE dangling reference.
findings.v2: Drops the default entirely: `[TicketNo] BIGINT NOT NULL,` (from:model reads defaults only from ossys Default_Value). Deploy -> exit 0, table created, ticketno_default=NONE: DEPLOYABLE but silently loses the sequence-backed default behavior.
findings.difference: Genuine DIVERGENCE. OutSystems does not model sequence defaults, so a DBA-added sequence default is invisible to the metamodel. v1 OVER-emits from physical reality and produces a bundle that fails to deploy (Msg 208); v2 UNDER-emits from the metamodel and produces a clean-but-lossy bundle (TICKETNO loses its auto-populate default; runtime inserts omitting TICKETNO would then hit NOT NULL). Both are wrong, in opposite directions.
findings.proof: v1b Ticket.sql:6 emits DEFAULT NEXT VALUE FOR [dbo].[TestSeq] with ossys Default_Value=NULL -> deploy Msg 208 (exit 1). v2b Ticket.sql:5 `[TicketNo] BIGINT NOT NULL,` (no default) -> deploy exit 0, sys.default_constraints readback NONE, seq_count=0.

findings.topic: Sequence-backed DEFAULT when the metamodel Default_Value carries the expression
findings.v1: Emits `CONSTRAINT [DF_OSUSR_SEQ_TICKET_TICKETNO] DEFAULT  NEXT VALUE FOR [dbo].[TestSeq]`. Deploy as-emitted -> Msg 208 (exit 1).
findings.v2: Emits `CONSTRAINT [DF_OSUSR_SEQ_TICKET_TICKETNO] DEFAULT NEXT VALUE FOR dbo.TestSeq`. Deploy as-emitted -> Msg 208 (exit 1).
findings.difference: Parity: when the ossys metamodel carries the sequence expression, BOTH engines emit the sequence-backed DEFAULT but NEITHER emits the sequence -> BOTH bundles are non-deployable with the identical Msg 208. Isolation proof: after pre-creating dbo.TestSeq in the target, BOTH deploy cleanly (exit 0), same table object_id 917578307, and both defaults resolve to the identical normalized (NEXT VALUE FOR [dbo].[TestSeq]). The ONLY missing artifact in both bundles is the CREATE SEQUENCE.
findings.proof: a-variant: both v1 and v2 dbo.Ticket.sql deploy -> Msg 208 'Invalid object name dbo.TestSeq' (exit 1). Isolation: CREATE SEQUENCE dbo.TestSeq then re-deploy -> both exit 0; readback ticket_default=(NEXT VALUE FOR [dbo].[TestSeq]) for both, seq_count=1.

findings.topic: Latent CREATE SEQUENCE emit capability (lane reachability)
findings.v1: No CREATE SEQUENCE emitter exists anywhere in v1 source. SequenceModel is populated only from JSON model input (ModelDocumentPipeline/ModuleFilter) and is never rendered to DDL by any emitter.
findings.v2: Full emit path present and wired: ScriptDomBuild.buildCreateSequence (fs:2631) builds a real CreateSequenceStatement, Statement.CreateSequence arm wired at ScriptDomBuild.fs:2717, Deploy.fs:475 handles it, fed by ReadSide.readSequences (sys.sequences). Exercised by the physical-reflection lane, not by from:model.
findings.difference: v2 could surface CREATE SEQUENCE via a different source lane (physical estate reflection); v1 cannot emit CREATE SEQUENCE at all under any lane without new code. So the parity in SEQ-01 masks an underlying capability asymmetry favouring v2.

findings.topic: Default-expression kind classification in the IR/snapshot
findings.v1: model.json stores the default as a plain string: attribute `.default` = 'NEXT VALUE FOR dbo.TestSeq' plus `.onDisk.defaultDefinition` = '(NEXT VALUE FOR [dbo].[TestSeq])' (no typed classification).

findings.proof: v2 catalog.snapshot.json:2765 `"kind": "IntegerLit", "value": "NEXT VALUE FOR dbo.TestSeq"`; emitted SQL still `DEFAULT NEXT VALUE FOR dbo.TestSeq` and deploys correctly.

findings.v2: `DEFAULT NEXT VALUE FOR dbo.TestSeq` — unbracketed schema.object, single space.

axis: deployed NOT NULL where the model says nullable (audit M-3 second half): a column marked Is_Mandatory=0 in the ossys model whose physical column is ALTERed to NOT NULL in the deployed DB — does v2 loosen to NULL (drop the deployed constraint) while v1 preserves NOT NULL via the physical-evidence path?
provisioned: DB [ParityAxis_deployednotnullmodelnullable] created + base OSSYS edge-case seed loaded (15 entities / 7 modules / 40 attrs). Axis feature: picked StockItem.AvgCost (ossys_Entity_Attr Id=60007; Is_Mandatory=0 → model-nullable; Is_Active=1; Data_Type=Float; no default/FK/computed) and ran `ALTER TABLE [dbo].[OSUSR_INV_STOCKITEM] ALTER COLUMN [AVGCOST] FLOAT NOT NULL`. Source truth confirmed via sys.columns: AVGCOST is_nullable flipped 1→0 (drift applied), table has 0 rows; model Is_Mandatory stays 0. Both CLIs ran clean: v1 full-export exit=0, v2 publish exit=0. All 3 databases (base + _V1 + _V2) dropped at end (confirmed gone).
v1Result: v1 full-export (default-tightening.json, EvidenceGated) emitted `[AvgCost]      FLOAT            NOT NULL` at line 10 of v1/out/Modules/Inventory/dbo.StockItem.sql. policy-decisions.json records the column decision {Schema:dbo, Table:OSUSR_INV_STOCKITEM, Column:AVGCOST, MakeNotNull:true, RequiresRemediation:false, Rationales:["PHYSICAL_NOT_NULL"]}; run.log summary: "2 attributes across 2 entities inherited NOT NULL status from the physical database schema". Deploy of that file to fresh DB _V1 succeeded (sqlcmd exit 0, no Msg errors); readback sys.columns.is_nullable=0 (NOT NULL). v1 PRESERVES the deployed constraint via its evidence path (PhysicalNotNullSignal S2_DB_NOT_NULL reads profiler ColumnProfile.IsNullablePhysical=false from the live schema).
v2Result: v2 publish (profiler live, default publish flow) emitted `[AvgCost]      FLOAT              NULL` at line 10 of v2/dist/onprem/Modules/Inventory/dbo.StockItem.sql. catalog.snapshot.json records the column as {"column":{"columnName":"AvgCost","isNullable":true},"isMandatory":false} — nullability is model-derived, physical drift invisible. Deploy of that file to fresh DB _V2 succeeded (sqlcmd exit 0, no Msg errors); readback sys.columns.is_nullable=1 (NULL). v2 LOOSENS: it drops the deployed NOT NULL and emits a nullable column, following the model's Is_Mandatory=0. No nullability diagnostic emitted for the column (NullabilityPass outcome is the no-signal keep-nullable path).

findings.topic: Deployed NOT NULL on a model-nullable column: v1 preserves, v2 loosens (emitted DDL + deployed reality)
findings.v1: [AvgCost] FLOAT NOT NULL; deployed sys.columns.is_nullable=0
findings.v2: [AvgCost] FLOAT NULL; deployed sys.columns.is_nullable=1
findings.difference: For a column the ossys model marks Is_Mandatory=0 but whose physical column was ALTERed to NOT NULL, v1 keeps the deployed NOT NULL (decision rationale PHYSICAL_NOT_NULL) while v2 silently emits NULL, dropping the deployed constraint and following the logical model. The two engines produce opposite target states for the exact same column; on an incremental apply against the drifted DB, v2 would drive the column back to NULL, weakening a production NOT NULL invariant with no diagnostic.
findings.proof: Source: sys.columns AVGCOST is_nullable=0 after ALTER. v1 dbo.StockItem.sql line10 `[AvgCost] FLOAT NOT NULL` + policy-decisions.json {MakeNotNull:true,Rationales:[PHYSICAL_NOT_NULL]}; deployed _V1 is_nullable=0. v2 dbo.StockItem.sql line10 `[AvgCost] FLOAT NULL`; deployed _V2 is_nullable=1. Both deploys sqlcmd exit 0, no Msg errors.

findings.topic: Root cause / capability-gap: v2 catalog nullability is model-derived, so its PhysicallyNotNull rule is blind to deployed drift
findings.v1: PhysicalNotNullSignal.cs:11 evaluates `context.ColumnProfile is { IsNullablePhysical: false }` — read from the live schema by the profiler, fires independently of model mandatoriness (rationale PHYSICAL_NOT_NULL).
findings.v2: OssysRowsetReader.fs:79 seeds Column.IsNullable = `not row.IsMandatory` (model only); NullabilityRules.fs:257 step 3 `elif not attribute.Column.IsNullable -> EnforceNotNull PhysicallyNotNull` reads that model-derived flag, never sys.columns.is_nullable.
findings.difference: v2 owns a nullability rule literally named PhysicallyNotNull, but because the catalog's Column.IsNullable comes from the model's Is_Mandatory rather than the physical schema, the rule can only fire when the model already says not-nullable — it is structurally incapable of observing a deployed NOT NULL on a model-nullable column. Consequently v2 cannot preserve the deployed constraint under ANY tightening direction/config: step 3 sees Column.IsNullable=true, step 4 (IsMandatory) is false, so it falls to step 5 KeepNullable(NoTighteningSignal) and keeps the model nullability. This is the mechanism behind DNN-1.
findings.proof: catalog.snapshot.json AvgCost {isNullable:true,isMandatory:false}. OssysRowsetReader.fs:79 `IsNullable = not row.IsMandatory`. NullabilityRules.fs:256-258 physical-not-null branch keys on `not attribute.Column.IsNullable`. Contrast v1 PhysicalNotNullSignal.cs:9-12 keys on profiler IsNullablePhysical. (v2 outcome-invariance to tightening config verified statically from the rule hierarchy; empirical run used the default publish flow.)

findings.topic: Controlled parity: every non-drifted column in the same table agrees on nullability
findings.v1: Id NOT NULL (PK), ReorderLevel/WarehouseQty NOT NULL, PublicGuid/LastSyncedAt/OpenTime NULL
findings.v2: identical nullability on all six columns
findings.difference: Within the same StockItem table, the only nullability divergence is AvgCost — the column I drifted. Model-mandatory columns (ReorderLevel, WarehouseQty, Id) emit NOT NULL on both; model-nullable/physically-null columns (PublicGuid, LastSyncedAt, OpenTime) emit NULL on both. This isolates the causal factor: the divergence is produced solely by the deployed-NOT-NULL vs model-nullable drift, with no other confounder.
findings.proof: Side-by-side CREATE TABLE (v1 vs v2): Id/ReorderLevel/WarehouseQty = NOT NULL on both; PublicGuid/LastSyncedAt/OpenTime = NULL on both; AvgCost = NOT NULL (v1) vs NULL (v2).

findings.topic: Boundary of the divergence class: a natural physically-NOT-NULL/model-nullable column (DISPLAYLABEL) is masked because both engines emit it computed
findings.v1: policy-decisions.json flags OSUSR_SAL_PRODUCT.DISPLAYLABEL with Rationales=[PHYSICAL_NOT_NULL] (computed PERSISTED, model non-mandatory, physically NOT NULL) — the 2nd of the 2 physical-schema-inherited NOT NULLs.

But both engines emit DISPLAYLABEL as a computed column (`[DisplayLabel] AS (concat([Sku],N'-',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(20),[Price])))`) with no NULL/NOT NULL token, so it yields no emitted nullability divergence. The v1-preserves/v2-loosens split therefore materializes only for non-computed scalar columns — AvgCost is the clean scalar witness.

findings.v2: [LastSyncedAt] DATETIMEOFFSET (7) NULL, [OpenTime] TIME (7) NULL (explicit scale 7)

axis: E7 — FK to a composite-PK target. OrderLine (entity 5003 / OSUSR_SAL_ORDERLINE) has composite PK (ORDERID, LINENO). New tables reference it via a single-column OutSystems reference attribute; test whether each engine emits the full composite FK or only the first leg (invalid SQL).
provisioned: DB ParityAxis_compositepkfk = base ossys-edge-case seed (15 entities/7 modules) + 2 new Sales entities referencing composite-PK OrderLine: (A) LineComment (5004/OSUSR_SAL_LINECOMMENT) — reference attr OrderLineId→OrderLine (bt-code bt4444…*bbbb…013, Protect) + plain LineNo(Integer) + Comment, backed by a PHYSICAL COMPOSITE FK FK_OSUSR_SAL_LINECOMMENT_ORDERLINE (ORDERLINEID,LINENO)→OSUSR_SAL_ORDERLINE(ORDERID,LINENO); (B) LineTag (5005/OSUSR_SAL_LINETAG) — reference attr OrderLineId→OrderLine (Protect) with NO physical FK (a single-col FK to OrderLine is un-seedable, ORDERID has no standalone unique index). Source truth confirmed via sys.foreign_key_columns: LineComment FK = 2 legs (ORDERLINEID→ORDERID ord1, LINENO→LINENO ord2); LineTag = 0 FKs. Both CLIs exited 0 (v1 full-export exit 0; v2 publish exit 0, 28 artifacts). Deploy targets ParityAxis_compositepkfk_V1 / _V2 each seeded with a matching [dbo].[OrderLine] (OrderId BIGINT, LineNo INT, PK(OrderId,LineNo)). All 3 DBs dropped at end.
v1Result: v1 (Osm.Cli full-export --profiler-provider sql) captured the physical composite FK in model.json (LineComment relationship→OrderLine, hasDbConstraint=1, actualConstraints=1 with 2 legs ordinals 1&2; LineTag hasDbConstraint=0, 0 actualConstraints). EMITTED DDL — LineComment (evidence path): table-level `CONSTRAINT [FK_LineComment_OrderLine_OrderLineId_LineNo] FOREIGN KEY ([OrderLineId], [LineNo]) REFERENCES [dbo].[OrderLine] ([OrderId], [LineNo])` = FULL COMPOSITE FK. LineTag (fallback path, no evidence): column-inline `CONSTRAINT [FK_LineTag_OrderLine_OrderLineId] FOREIGN KEY ([OrderLineId]) REFERENCES [dbo].[OrderLine] ([OrderId])` = SINGLE FIRST LEG. DEPLOY into _V1: LineComment.sql deployed CLEAN (sys.foreign_keys shows FK_LineComment_OrderLine_OrderLineId_LineNo with 2 legs persisted; table present); LineTag.sql FAILED — Msg 1776 "There are no primary or candidate keys in the referenced table 'dbo.OrderLine' that match the referencing column list in the foreign key 'FK_LineTag_OrderLine_OrderLineId'." + Msg 1750 "Could not create constraint or index" (table not created).
v2Result: v2 (projection.dll publish --json, profiler live) recorded the reference as a single-column IR node — catalog.snapshot.json Reference = { sourceAttribute: O32:…OrderLineId, targetKind: O32:…OrderLine, hasDbConstraint:true (LineComment) / false (LineTag) } with NO referenced-column list and NO second leg; the composite reality is structurally absent from v2's model. EMITTED DDL — BOTH LineComment AND LineTag identical: column-inline `CONSTRAINT [FK_<T>_OrderLine_OrderLineId] FOREIGN KEY ([OrderLineId]) REFERENCES [dbo].[OrderLine] ([OrderId])` = SINGLE FIRST LEG in both, including the case where the source had a valid composite FK. No warning/refusal/remediation about the dropped leg anywhere (run.log, fidelity.txt, manifest.remediation.sql). DEPLOY into _V2: BOTH LineComment.sql and LineTag.sql FAILED — each Msg 1776 (…no primary or candidate keys in 'dbo.OrderLine' that match … foreign key 'FK_LineComment_OrderLine_OrderLineId' / 'FK_LineTag_OrderLine_OrderLineId') + Msg 1750; neither table created (only OrderLine present in _V2, zero FKs).

findings.topic: Source has a physical COMPOSITE FK to composite-PK OrderLine (LineComment): v2 emits only the first leg → undeployable; v1 emits the full composite FK → deploys
findings.v1: Full composite FK: FOREIGN KEY ([OrderLineId],[LineNo]) REFERENCES [dbo].[OrderLine]([OrderId],[LineNo]); deploys clean, 2-leg FK persisted in _V1.
findings.v2: Single first leg only: FOREIGN KEY ([OrderLineId]) REFERENCES [dbo].[OrderLine]([OrderId]); deploy fails Msg 1776 + Msg 1750; LineComment table not created.
findings.difference: v1's model.json carries the physical FK as a 2-leg actualConstraint and the ForeignKeyEvidenceResolver emits both legs (referenced side = OrderLine's real PK (OrderId,LineNo)). v2's Reference IR is single sourceAttribute→targetKind with no referenced-column list, so despite recording hasDbConstraint=true it drops the LineNo leg and collapses the referenced side to OrderLine's FIRST PK column (OrderId) — which is not a standalone key, so the FK is invalid.
findings.proof: v2 _V2 deploy: Msg 1776 'There are no primary or candidate keys in the referenced table dbo.OrderLine that match the referencing column list in the foreign key FK_LineComment_OrderLine_OrderLineId' + Msg 1750; v1 _V1 readback: FK_LineComment_OrderLine_OrderLineId_LineNo present with legs (OrderLineId→OrderId ord1, LineNo→LineNo ord2). v1 model.json LineComment actualConstraints=1 with 2 legs; v2 catalog.snapshot Reference has only sourceAttribute+targetKind.

findings.topic: Source has NO physical FK, only a logical single-column reference to composite-PK OrderLine (LineTag): both engines synthesize an invalid single-leg FK
findings.v1: FOREIGN KEY ([OrderLineId]) REFERENCES [dbo].[OrderLine]([OrderId]) (fallback = GetPreferredIdentifier, single col); Msg 1776 + 1750 on deploy; table not created.
findings.v2: Byte-identical single-leg FK FOREIGN KEY ([OrderLineId]) REFERENCES [dbo].[OrderLine]([OrderId]); Msg 1776 + 1750 on deploy; table not created.
findings.difference: With no physical-FK evidence, both engines fabricate an FK from the single-column OutSystems reference against the target's first PK column and neither checks that OrderLine's PK is composite. Both produce undeployable SQL — this is a SHARED correctness defect, not a v2 regression (v1's fallback path == v2's only path).
findings.proof: Both _V1 (LineTag.sql) and _V2 (LineTag.sql) deploys emit Msg 1776 for FK_LineTag_OrderLine_OrderLineId + Msg 1750; LineTag absent from both target DBs. Emitted FK text identical across engines.

findings.topic: v2 reduces a composite FK to a single leg with no diagnostic (violates downgrades-never-silent)
findings.v1: n/a for LineComment (v1 emits the composite FK correctly); for the LineTag single-leg case v1 likewise emits with no remediation note.
findings.v2: Recorded hasDbConstraint=true for LineComment yet emitted a 1-leg FK; zero warn/refusal/remediation about the lost LineNo leg in run.log, fidelity.txt, manifest.remediation.sql (only unrelated nullability/type + JobRun-cycle warnings present).
findings.difference: The truncation of a valid 2-leg physical FK to an invalid 1-leg FK is lossy and silent in v2 — no operator signal that emitted SQL will fail to deploy.
findings.proof: grep of v2 run.log/fidelity/remediation for FK/composite/OrderLine/1776/refus/degrade found no truncation diagnostic; v2 publish exited 0 while emitting undeployable DDL.

findings.topic: Constraint naming + placement differ as a side effect of leg count
findings.v1: Composite case names the FK with both columns and places it table-level: CONSTRAINT [FK_LineComment_OrderLine_OrderLineId_LineNo] on its own line.
findings.v2: Always single-column name, column-inline: CONSTRAINT [FK_LineComment_OrderLine_OrderLineId] attached under [OrderLineId].
findings.difference: Direct consequence of v1 emitting 2 legs vs v2 emitting 1; not an independent defect but a visible naming/format divergence when a composite FK is involved. For the single-leg LineTag case the names/placement match between engines.
findings.proof: v1 LineComment: table-level 'CONSTRAINT [FK_LineComment_OrderLine_OrderLineId_LineNo] FOREIGN KEY ([OrderLineId], [LineNo])'; v2 LineComment: inline 'CONSTRAINT [FK_LineComment_OrderLine_OrderLineId] FOREIGN KEY ([OrderLineId])'.

axis: >1000-row static entity data lane: v2 data-staging #temp escalation (F7, threshold 1000) vs v1 >1000-row Authoritative batch-boundary row-loss claim. Both CLIs exited 0: v1 full-export (NonDestructive default) exit 0; v1 full-export (Authoritative variant, tightening emission.staticSeeds.mode=Authoritative) exit 0; v2 publish --json exit 0. v1Result: v1 emits Seeds/RefData/StaticEntities.seed.sql. StaticSeedSqlBuilder.cs: DefaultBatchSize=1000, requiresBatching = rows.Length > 1000 -> 1500 rows PARTITIONED into 2 batches (1000+500), each a SEPARATE inline `MERGE INTO [dbo].[Country] AS Target USING (VALUES ...) AS Source ([Id],[Code],[Name]) ON Target.[Id]=Source.[Id]`, GO-separated, NOT wrapped in any transaction. Header: '-- Note: Large table with 1500 rows will be processed in batches of 1000'; per-batch PRINT 'Applying batch 1/2 ... (1000 rows)' / 'Applying batch 2/2 ... (500 rows)'. Target is the LOGICAL name [dbo].[Country] (not physical OSUSR_REF_COUNTRY); table DDL Id BIGINT IDENTITY PK, Code NVARCHAR(3), Name NVARCHAR(100); bracketed by SET IDENTITY_INSERT ON/OFF. MODE-DEPENDENT tail: NonDestructive (default) each MERGE ends `;` (0 delete arms); Authoritative each MERGE ends `WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE THEN DELETE;` (2 delete arms, seed lines 1037 & 1554). DEPLOY (ddl exit 0 / seed exit 0, 0 Msg both): NonDestructive -> dbo.Country COUNT=1500 MIN=1 MAX=1500 (exact). Authoritative -> dbo.Country COUNT=500 MIN=1001 MAX=1500 -- SILENT loss of 1000 rows (deploy stdout was ONLY the two PRINT lines, no Msg/error): batch-1's 1000 rows (Id 1-1000) inserted, then batch-2's MERGE WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE THEN DELETE deleted them because Id 1-1000 are absent from batch-2's 500-row VALUES source; only batch-2 rows (Id 1001-1500) survive.
v2Result: v2 publish emits ONE data file Data/StaticSeeds.sql (Country only; empty CurrencyCode contributes nothing). shouldStage(auto,1500) = 1500 > Threshold(1000) = true -> ESCALATED to staged #temp form (StagedMerge.renderStagedPhase1). Structure (1530 lines, single GO-terminated batch): line1 `SET XACT_ABORT ON;`, `BEGIN TRY`, `BEGIN TRANSACTION;`, `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS [#seed_Country];`, `CREATE TABLE [#seed_Country] ([Id] BIGINT NULL,[Code] NVARCHAR(3) NULL,[Name] NVARCHAR(100) NULL);` (all-nullable staging), then TWO temp-INSERT batches (INSERT [#seed_Country] ... VALUES at line 11 with 1000 rows + line 1012 with 500 rows = all 1500 accumulate in ONE temp), then ONE `SET IDENTITY_INSERT [dbo].[Country] ON; MERGE INTO [dbo].[Country] AS [Target] USING [#seed_Country] AS [Source] ON [Target].[Id]=[Source].[Id] WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE ... WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT ...;` (upsert-only, no delete arm by default), `SET IDENTITY_INSERT OFF; DROP TABLE [#seed_Country]; COMMIT TRANSACTION; END TRY BEGIN CATCH ... ROLLBACK; THROW; END CATCH; GO`. Target logical name [dbo].[Country], Id BIGINT IDENTITY PK (same as v1) + v2-only provenance extended properties (Projection.LogicalName/SsKey). DEPLOY (ddl exit 0 / seed exit 0, 0 Msg): dbo.Country COUNT=1500 MIN=1 MAX=1500 (exact). The single MERGE over the fully-staged 1500-row #temp has NO batch boundary in the MERGE step, so it is structurally immune to v1's Authoritative loss even were a delete arm configured (the source would hold all 1500 rows).

findings.topic: Batch-boundary row survival for a >1000-row static entity under destructive (Authoritative / WHEN-NOT-MATCHED-BY-SOURCE-DELETE) sync
findings.v1: Authoritative mode: 2 independent inline MERGEs (1000+500 rows), EACH carrying its own WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE THEN DELETE;. Batch-2's delete arm deletes batch-1's rows. Deployed dbo.Country COUNT=500, MIN=1001, MAX=1500 -- 1000 of 1500 rows silently lost (deploy exit 0, 0 Msg, only PRINT lines).
findings.v2: Single staged MERGE over one #seed_Country temp holding all 1500 rows. Deployed COUNT=1500, MIN=1, MAX=1500. Architecturally immune: the one MERGE always sees the full row set, so a WHEN-NOT-MATCHED-BY-SOURCE arm could never delete rows that belong.
findings.difference: v1's per-batch MERGE partitioning makes destructive sync (Authoritative) a silent data-corruption bug on any static entity above 1000 rows: each batch's delete arm nukes prior batches, leaving only the final batch (here 500/1500 = 67% loss). v2's stage-all-then-one-MERGE design cannot exhibit this.
findings.proof: Deployed row counts: v1-Authoritative dbo.Country COUNT=500 MIN=1001 MAX=1500 (exactly batch-2's Id range) vs source 1500 and v2 1500. Seed source: StaticSeedSqlBuilder.cs:95 requiresBatching=rows>1000, :169 PartitionRows(1000), :261-263 appends WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE THEN DELETE; per batch; emitted arm confirmed at seed lines 1037 & 1554. Loss is silent: v1auth seed deploy stdout = only 'Applying batch 1/2 (1000 rows)' + 'Applying batch 2/2 (500 rows)'.

findings.topic: v2 data-staging #temp escalation (F7) at row threshold 1000
findings.v1: No staging concept: emits N inline MERGE ... USING (VALUES ...) batches (batch size 1000) directly against the live target; no #temp, no transaction envelope.
findings.v2: 1500 > DataStagingPolicy.auto.Threshold(1000) -> escalates to a #seed_Country #temp (rows bulk-loaded via <=1000-row INSERT batches) followed by a SINGLE MERGE INTO [dbo].[Country] USING [#seed_Country], the whole unit inside SET XACT_ABORT ON + BEGIN TRY/BEGIN TRANSACTION ... COMMIT / BEGIN CATCH ROLLBACK; THROW.
findings.difference: The F7 escalation claim is EMPIRICALLY CONFIRMED for v2: threshold is exactly 1000 (rowCount>1000), it stages through #temp, and the MERGE runs inside an atomic XACT_ABORT TRY/TRAN. v1 has no equivalent escalation.
findings.proof: Policy.fs:164 auto={Auto;Threshold=1000;IndexThreshold=100000}; :173 Auto->rowCount>Threshold. Emitted StaticSeeds.sql line1 SET XACT_ABORT ON;, line3-4 BEGIN TRY/BEGIN TRANSACTION, line5-6 DROP/CREATE [#seed_Country], lines 11 & 1012 two INSERT batches into the temp, line1514 single MERGE INTO [dbo].[Country] ... USING [#seed_Country], line1523 COMMIT, line1525-1528 CATCH/ROLLBACK/THROW. 1500 value tuples staged (1498 tuple-lines + 2 VALUES-header rows).

findings.topic: All-or-nothing atomicity of the data load
findings.v1: Batches are GO-separated and NOT wrapped in a transaction. A mid-load failure (e.g. batch 2 fails) leaves batch 1 already committed -> partial/torn apply. IDENTITY_INSERT ON/OFF also straddle multiple GO batches.
findings.v2: Entire load (temp create + inserts + MERGE + drop) is one SET XACT_ABORT ON TRY/TRAN unit committed once; any error hits BEGIN CATCH -> ROLLBACK + THROW, and the #temp is transactional so it is never leaked.
findings.difference: v2 gives transactional all-or-nothing semantics; v1's non-transactional GO-batched MERGEs can leave a static table half-loaded on failure. Independent of the F1 delete-arm bug -- this is a plain atomicity gap.
findings.proof: v1 seed: SET IDENTITY_INSERT [dbo].[Country] ON;\nGO then per-batch MERGE ...;\nGO (no BEGIN TRAN anywhere in the file). v2 seed: single BEGIN TRANSACTION ... COMMIT TRANSACTION wrapped by BEGIN TRY/BEGIN CATCH ROLLBACK;THROW (lines 3-4, 1523, 1525-1528).

findings.topic: Default (non-destructive) static-seed lane row fidelity
findings.v1: NonDestructive (the config default, StaticSeedSynchronizationMode enum value 0): 2 upsert-only MERGE batches, 0 delete arms -> deployed COUNT=1500 MIN=1 MAX=1500.
findings.v2: Default publish (upsert-only staged MERGE, no delete arm) -> deployed COUNT=1500 MIN=1 MAX=1500.
findings.difference: On the DEFAULT posture both lanes load all 1500 rows correctly -- full parity. The divergence (F1) surfaces only when the operator selects v1's Authoritative destructive mode; v1's default is safe.
findings.proof: Deployed counts: v1-NonDestructive dbo.Country COUNT=1500 MIN=1 MAX=1500; v2 dbo.Country COUNT=1500 MIN=1 MAX=1500; source OSUSR_REF_COUNTRY=1500. default-tightening.json emission.staticSeeds.mode='NonDestructive'; TighteningOptions.cs:388 StaticSeedOptions.Default = NonDestructive.

findings.topic: The row-count at which each engine changes strategy
findings.v1: StaticSeedSqlBuilder DefaultBatchSize=1000; switches to multi-batch when rows > 1000 (to stay under SQL Server's 1000-row VALUES table-constructor ceiling).
findings.v2: DataStagingPolicy.auto.Threshold=1000; switches to #temp staging when rowCount > 1000 (to avoid the MERGE error-8623 plan-complexity wall).
findings.difference: Both engines pivot at the SAME numeric threshold (1000, strictly greater) so 1500 triggers both -- but for DIFFERENT reasons (v1: VALUES ceiling; v2: 8623 wall) and via different mechanisms (v1 splits the MERGE; v2 stages the rows). Threshold value is at parity; the mechanism is not.
findings.proof: v1 StaticSeedSqlBuilder.cs:14 DefaultBatchSize=1000, :95 rows.Length>_batchSize. v2 Policy.fs:164 Threshold=1000, :173 rowCount>Threshold. Both fired at 1500 (v1 emitted 'batches of 1000'; v2 emitted #seed_Country stage).

findings.topic: Deploy target identity, key type, and provenance annotations
findings.v1: Emits table + seed against LOGICAL name [dbo].[Country] (not physical OSUSR_REF_COUNTRY); Id mapped to BIGINT IDENTITY(1,1) PK; no extended properties beyond MS_Description.
findings.v2: Same logical target [dbo].[Country], same Id BIGINT IDENTITY(1,1) PK; ADDITIONALLY emits Projection.LogicalName + Projection.SsKey extended properties on the table and every column (e.g. SsKey O32:bbbbbbbb...040).
findings.difference: Target table name and key type are at full parity (both retarget to the logical Country/BIGINT). v2 additionally carries provenance extended-properties the v1 output lacks -- off the data-lane axis but present in the deployed schema; both deployed with 0 Msg.
findings.proof: v1 dbo.Country.sql and v2 dbo.Country.sql both: [Id] BIGINT IDENTITY (1, 1) NOT NULL CONSTRAINT [PK_Country_Id] PRIMARY KEY CLUSTERED, [Code] NVARCHAR(3) NOT NULL, [Name] NVARCHAR(100) NOT NULL. v2 adds sp_addextendedproperty Projection.LogicalName/Projection.SsKey per table+column. Both DDL deploys exit 0, 0 Msg.

provisioned: DB ParityAxis_scalarfacets = base ossys-edge-case seed (15 entities / 7 modules / 2 static kinds) + 1 added entity ScalarFacet (module RefData, physical dbo.OSUSR_REF_SCALARFACET) whose 7 attrs deliberately probe NON-DEFAULT temporal scales + precision so an emitted facet can be distinguished from a hardcoded default: Id (Identifier; physical INT IDENTITY), Dt2Lo (datetime2, ossys Scale=3; physical DATETIME2(3)), TimeLo (time, Scale=2; physical TIME(2)), DtoLo (datetimeoffset, Scale=4; physical DATETIMEOFFSET(4)), DecOdd (Decimal, Precision=28 Scale=10; physical DECIMAL(28,10)), LegacyDt (DateTime; physical genuine DATETIME), Cur (Currency; physical DECIMAL(37,8)). ossys_Entity_Attr rows mirror the seed's second-block column shape. Source truth confirmed via sys.columns before running. Both CLIs exited 0: v1 full-export (--profiler-provider sql) exit 0; v2 publish (profiler live) reported 12/12 stages succeeded, 0 error-level events. Deploy targets ParityAxis_scalarfacets_V1 / _V2 each created 16 tables. All three DBs dropped at end (confirmed none remain).
v1Result: v1 full-export emitted per-table DDL under Modules/. TEMPORAL types emitted WITHOUT any scale token: `[Dt2Lo] DATETIME2 NOT NULL`, `[TimeLo] TIME NULL`, `[DtoLo] DATETIMEOFFSET NULL`, `[LastSyncedAt] DATETIMEOFFSET`, `[OpenTime] TIME`, `[PlacedAt] DATETIME2`, `[MovedAt] DATETIME2`, `[CreatedOn] DATETIME2`. xml → `[RawXml] NVARCHAR (MAX)`. Email → `[Email] NVARCHAR (255) NOT NULL` (no COLLATE). Identifier/PK/FK → `BIGINT IDENTITY (1, 1)`. Currency → `DECIMAL (37, 8)`. Decimal → `DECIMAL (28, 10)` and `DECIMAL (18, 2)`. Date → `DATE`. Float → `FLOAT`. guid → `UNIQUEIDENTIFIER`. BinaryData → `VARBINARY (MAX)`. Text(n) → `NVARCHAR (40/100/255/…)`. Legacy datetime → `DATETIME`. Deploy to ParityAxis_scalarfacets_V1: exit 0, clean, 16 tables. Crucial: v1's extract-out model.json DID capture the scales correctly (Dt2Lo dataType.scale=3 & onDisk.scale=3; TimeLo=2; DtoLo=4) — the scale is dropped at the DDL text-rendering layer, not at extraction (decimal precision/scale IS rendered, e.g. DECIMAL(28,10)).
v2Result: v2 publish emitted per-table DDL under dist/onprem/Modules/. TEMPORAL types emitted WITH explicit scale: `[Dt2Lo] DATETIME2 (3)`, `[TimeLo] TIME (2)`, `[DtoLo] DATETIMEOFFSET (4)`, `[LastSyncedAt] DATETIMEOFFSET (7)`, `[OpenTime] TIME (7)`, `[PlacedAt] DATETIME2 (7)`, `[MovedAt] DATETIME2 (7)`, `[CreatedOn] DATETIME2 (7)`. xml → `[RawXml] XML`. Email → `[Email] NVARCHAR (255) COLLATE Latin1_General_CI_AI NOT NULL`. Identifier/PK/FK → `BIGINT IDENTITY (1, 1)`. Currency → `DECIMAL (37, 8)`. Decimal → `DECIMAL (28, 10)` / `DECIMAL (18, 2)`. Date → `DATE`. Float → `FLOAT`. guid → `UNIQUEIDENTIFIER`. BinaryData → `VARBINARY (MAX)`. Text(n) → `NVARCHAR (n)`. Legacy datetime → `DATETIME`. Deploy to ParityAxis_scalarfacets_V2: exit 0; the ONLY errors were trigger objects (Msg 8197 + Msg 4920 on TR_OSUSR_INV_MOVEMENT_INS/UPD and TR_OSUSR_XYZ_JOBRUN_AUDIT) — trigger axis, not type-facet; all 16 tables created. Off-axis observation: v2 rendered JobRun's getutcdate() default as `CAST ('getutcdate()' AS DATETIME2 (7))` (default-expression axis, not scored here).

findings.topic: Temporal scale facet (datetime2 / time / datetimeoffset) round-trip
findings.v1: Emits the base type WITHOUT a scale token (`DATETIME2`, `TIME`, `DATETIMEOFFSET`) -> SQL Server applies default scale 7 on deploy, regardless of source scale
findings.v2: Emits the explicit on-disk scale (`DATETIME2 (3)`, `TIME (2)`, `DATETIMEOFFSET (4)`, `... (7)`) -> deploys at exact source scale
findings.difference: V1 drops the scale facet for temporal types at the DDL-rendering layer; V2 preserves it. When source scale = 7 (the OutSystems platform default) the two are deploy-equivalent (bare == 7), which hides the defect on ordinary estates. For ANY non-default temporal scale V1 silently widens the deployed column to scale 7 (schema-type + storage-byte drift vs source), while V2 round-trips faithfully. Failure scenario: an estate with TIME(3)/DATETIME2(3) audit timestamps -> V1 redeploys them as scale 7, a silent schema divergence a schema-compare / idempotent redeploy would flag.
findings.proof: ScalarFacet probe, source vs deployed sys.columns.scale -- Dt2Lo: source datetime2 scale=3 -> V1 deployed scale=7 (maxlen 8), V2 deployed scale=3 (maxlen 7). TimeLo: source time scale=2 -> V1 scale=7, V2 scale=2. DtoLo: source datetimeoffset scale=4 -> V1 scale=7, V2 scale=4. v1/model.json carried onDisk.scale=3/2/4 (extraction correct) yet emitted bare `DATETIME2`/`TIME`/`DATETIMEOFFSET`. Base-estate scale-7 cols (LastSyncedAt/OpenTime/PlacedAt/MovedAt/CreatedOn) deployed scale=7 on BOTH.

findings.topic: xml scalar type round-trip
findings.v1: `[RawXml] NVARCHAR (MAX)` -> deploys as nvarchar(max)
findings.v2: `[RawXml] XML` -> deploys as xml
findings.difference: Source column is native `xml`; v2's config maps xml->xml AND the on-disk reflection confirms it, so V2 preserves the XML type. V1 downgrades it to NVARCHAR(MAX), losing the XML type contract (typed storage, .value()/.query()/.nodes() methods, XML indexes). Data text is preserved but the semantic type is lost. Failure scenario: an Integration/external entity xml column -> code using XML methods or XML indexes breaks against V1's deployed schema.
findings.proof: SyncLog.RawXml -- source sys.types=xml; V1 deployed ty=nvarchar maxlen=-1; V2 deployed ty=xml maxlen=-1. v1 emitted `[RawXml] NVARCHAR (MAX)`, v2 emitted `[RawXml] XML`.

findings.topic: Collation facet on NVARCHAR columns
findings.v1: Omits the COLLATE clause -> column falls to database default collation (SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS)
findings.v2: Emits `COLLATE Latin1_General_CI_AI` -> preserves the source collation
findings.difference: Customer.Email source is nvarchar(255) COLLATE Latin1_General_CI_AI (accent-INsensitive). V2 preserves it; V1 drops it, so the deployed column uses the DB-default accent-SENSITIVE collation, changing equality/sort/uniqueness semantics. Adjacent to the scalar-type axis (collation is a char-type facet). Failure scenario: a deliberately accent/case-insensitive key column silently becomes case/accent-sensitive under V1.
findings.proof: Customer.Email deployed sys.columns.collation_name: V1=SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS (DB default), V2=Latin1_General_CI_AI (matches source). v1 emitted `[Email] NVARCHAR (255) NOT NULL`; v2 emitted `[Email] NVARCHAR (255) COLLATE Latin1_General_CI_AI NOT NULL`.

findings.topic: DateTime logical attr backed by physical datetime2(7): emitted-text diff, deploy-equivalent
findings.v1: `DATETIME2` (bare) -> deploys datetime2(7)
findings.v2: `DATETIME2 (7)` -> deploys datetime2(7)
findings.difference: Both engines prefer the on-disk/deployed datetime2 over the `datetime` logical mapping (on-disk precedence), so the DEPLOYED reality is identical (scale 7 = source). Only the emitted DDL TEXT differs -- V1 omits the default-7 token. This is the latent, deploy-equivalent form of SF-1; it becomes a functional divergence only when the physical scale is non-default.
findings.proof: SalesOrder.PlacedAt / StockMovement.MovedAt / JobRun.CreatedOn -- v1 emitted bare `DATETIME2`, v2 emitted `DATETIME2 (7)`; both deployed datetime2 scale=7, equal to source datetime2(7).

findings.topic: Identifier / autonumber / reference -> BIGINT vs physical INT
findings.v1: `BIGINT IDENTITY (1, 1)` -- overrides physical INT (ShouldPreferRuntimeMapping)
findings.v2: `BIGINT IDENTITY (1, 1)` -- overrides physical INT (WP-4b register C2: forced-runtime family exempt from deployed-storage refinement)
findings.difference: Both engines force identifier/autonumber/longinteger/reference columns to BIGINT (OutSystems runtime convention), deliberately NOT following the physical INT. Engine-to-engine PARITY; both intentionally diverge from source-physical (a widening INT->BIGINT redefinition every identity + FK column undergoes on deploy).
findings.proof: Every PK Id + FK column -- source sys.columns ty=int precision=10; V1 deployed=bigint precision=19; V2 deployed=bigint precision=19 (e.g. Product.Id, Customer.CityId, SalesOrder.CustomerId/CategoryId, StockMovement.StockItemId, JobRun.TriggeredByUserId). Both emitted BIGINT IDENTITY(1,1).

findings.topic: Currency -> DECIMAL(37,8) and legacy DateTime -> DATETIME
findings.v1: Currency=`DECIMAL (37, 8)`; genuine legacy datetime=`DATETIME`
findings.v2: Currency=`DECIMAL (37, 8)`; genuine legacy datetime=`DATETIME`
findings.difference: Two axis-focus questions answered with PARITY: (a) Currency maps to DECIMAL(37,8) on both; (b) a column whose physical type is genuinely legacy `datetime` stays DATETIME on both -- neither engine silently upgrades legacy datetime to datetime2 (v2's WP-17(d) retired that silent upgrade; v1 maps datetime->datetime).
findings.proof: Currency: Product.Cost / SalesOrder.OrderTotal / ScalarFacet.Cur -- both emitted `DECIMAL (37, 8)`, both deployed precision=37 scale=8. Legacy datetime: ScalarFacet.LegacyDt source=datetime -> both emitted `DATETIME`, both deployed ty=datetime.

findings.topic: Non-temporal scalar mapping parity (int / bigint / bit / decimal(p,s) / date / float / guid / varbinary(max) / nvarchar(n|max))
findings.v1: INT / BIGINT / BIT / DECIMAL(p,s) / DATE / FLOAT / UNIQUEIDENTIFIER / VARBINARY(MAX) / NVARCHAR(n|MAX)
findings.v2: byte-identical to v1
findings.difference: Every non-temporal, non-xml scalar type maps identically and deploys identically on both engines: Integer->INT, LongInteger->BIGINT, Boolean->BIT, Decimal(18,2)->DECIMAL(18,2), Decimal(28,10)->DECIMAL(28,10), Date->DATE, Float->FLOAT, guid->UNIQUEIDENTIFIER, BinaryData->VARBINARY(MAX) (length always MAX -- OutSystems BinaryData has no bounded form), Text(n)->NVARCHAR(n), Text(no length)->NVARCHAR(MAX).
findings.proof: Deployed sys.columns identical on both DBs -- StockItem.ReorderLevel int, WarehouseQty bigint, AvgCost float(53), PublicGuid uniqueidentifier; Product.Price decimal(18,2), LaunchDate date, Photo varbinary(-1), Notes nvarchar(-1), Sku nvarchar(80=40 chars); ScalarFacet.DecOdd decimal(28,10); SyncLog.Payload nvarchar(-1); City.IsActive bit DEFAULT 1.
